From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Greatest films ever made](/w/index.php?title=Greatest_films_ever_made&redirect=no "Greatest films ever made"))

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/34/Citizen-Kane-Welles-Podium.jpg/250px-Citizen-Kane-Welles-Podium.jpg)](/wiki/File:Citizen-Kane-Welles-Podium.jpg)

*[Citizen Kane](/wiki/Citizen_Kane "Citizen Kane")* (1941), starring and directed by [Orson Welles](/wiki/Orson_Welles "Orson Welles"), has topped several international polls, including five consecutive decades at number 1 in the [British Film Institute](/wiki/British_Film_Institute "British Film Institute")'s *[Sight and Sound](/wiki/Sight_and_Sound "Sight and Sound")* decennial poll of critics.

The following films have been voted the best in national and international [surveys](/wiki/Opinion_poll "Opinion poll") of [critics](/wiki/Film_criticism "Film criticism") and the public. Some surveys focus on all films, while others focus on a particular genre or country. [Voting systems](/wiki/Electoral_system "Electoral system") differ, and some surveys suffer from biases such as [self-selection](/wiki/Self-selection_bias "Self-selection bias") or skewed [demographics](/wiki/Demography "Demography"), while others may be susceptible to forms of interference such as [vote stacking](/wiki/Vote_stacking "Vote stacking").

## Critics and filmmakers

### *Sight and Sound*

Main articles: [Sight and Sound](/wiki/Sight_and_Sound "Sight and Sound") and [The Sight and Sound Greatest Films of All Time 2022](/wiki/The_Sight_and_Sound_Greatest_Films_of_All_Time_2022 "The Sight and Sound Greatest Films of All Time 2022")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e5/Vertigo_1958_trailer_Kim_Novak_at_Golden_Gate_Bridge_Fort_Point.jpg/250px-Vertigo_1958_trailer_Kim_Novak_at_Golden_Gate_Bridge_Fort_Point.jpg)](/wiki/File:Vertigo_1958_trailer_Kim_Novak_at_Golden_Gate_Bridge_Fort_Point.jpg)

[*Vertigo*](/wiki/Vertigo_(film) "Vertigo (film)") (1958) was ranked number one in the 2012 [British Film Institute](/wiki/British_Film_Institute "British Film Institute")'s *[Sight and Sound](/wiki/Sight_and_Sound "Sight and Sound")* decennial poll of critics and number two in their 2022 poll.

Every decade, starting in 1952, the British film magazine *Sight and Sound* asks an international group of film critics to vote for the greatest film of all time. Since 1992, they have invited directors to vote in a separate poll. Sixty-three critics participated in 1952, 70 critics in 1962, 89 critics in 1972, 122 critics in 1982, 132 critics and 101 directors in 1992, 145 critics and 108 directors in 2002, 846 critics and 358 directors in 2012, and 1639 critics and 480 directors in 2022.[[1]](#cite_note-S&S-1)

This poll is regarded as one of the most important "greatest ever film" lists. The American critic [Roger Ebert](/wiki/Roger_Ebert "Roger Ebert") described it as "by far the most respected of the countless polls of great movies—the only one most serious movie people take seriously".[[2]](#cite_note-2)

* *[Bicycle Thieves](/wiki/Bicycle_Thieves "Bicycle Thieves")* (1948) topped the first poll in 1952 with 25 votes.[[1]](#cite_note-S&S-1)
* *[Citizen Kane](/wiki/Citizen_Kane "Citizen Kane")* (1941) stood at number 1 for five consecutive polls, with 22 votes in 1962, 32 votes in 1972, 45 votes in 1982, 43 votes in 1992, and 46 votes in 2002. It also topped the first two directors' polls, with 30 votes in 1992 and 42 votes in 2002.[[1]](#cite_note-S&S-1)
* *[Vertigo](/wiki/Vertigo_(film) "Vertigo (film)")* (1958) topped [the critics' poll in 2012](/wiki/The_Sight_%26_Sound_Greatest_Films_of_All_Time_2012 "The Sight & Sound Greatest Films of All Time 2012") with 191 votes, dethroning *Citizen Kane*.[[1]](#cite_note-S&S-1)
* *[Tokyo Story](/wiki/Tokyo_Story "Tokyo Story")* (1953) topped the directors' poll in 2012 with 48 votes, also dethroning *Citizen Kane*.[[1]](#cite_note-S&S-1)
* *[Jeanne Dielman, 23 quai du Commerce, 1080 Bruxelles](/wiki/Jeanne_Dielman,_23_quai_du_Commerce,_1080_Bruxelles "Jeanne Dielman, 23 quai du Commerce, 1080 Bruxelles")* (1975) topped the critics' poll in 2022.[[1]](#cite_note-S&S-1)
* [*2001: A Space Odyssey*](/wiki/2001:_A_Space_Odyssey_(film) "2001: A Space Odyssey (film)") (1968) topped the directors' poll in 2022.[[1]](#cite_note-S&S-1)

### Other polls

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/df/The-Nihon-Eiiga-1953-August-3.jpg/250px-The-Nihon-Eiiga-1953-August-3.jpg)](/wiki/File:The-Nihon-Eiiga-1953-August-3.jpg)

*[Seven Samurai](/wiki/Seven_Samurai "Seven Samurai")* (1954) topped the [BBC](/wiki/BBC "BBC") poll of best foreign-language films as well as several Japanese polls.

* *[Battleship Potemkin](/wiki/Battleship_Potemkin "Battleship Potemkin")* (1925) was ranked number 1 with 32 votes when the Festival Mondial du Film et des Beaux-Arts de Belgique asked 63 film professionals around the world, mostly directors, to vote for the best films of the half-century in 1951.[[3]](#cite_note-Referendum_International-3) It was also ranked number 1 when the [Brussels World's Fair](/wiki/Expo_58 "Expo 58") polled 117 experts from 26 countries in 1958.[[4]](#cite_note-Expo_58-4)
* *[Citizen Kane](/wiki/Citizen_Kane "Citizen Kane")* (1941) was ranked number 1 with 48 votes when French film magazine *[Cahiers du cinéma](/wiki/Cahiers_du_cin%C3%A9ma "Cahiers du cinéma")* asked 78 French critics and historians to vote for the best films in 2007.[[5]](#cite_note-5) It was also ranked number 1 with 48 votes when Chinese website Cinephilia.net asked 135 Chinese-speaking critics, scholars, curators, and cultural workers to vote for the best films in 2012.[[6]](#cite_note-6) It was ranked number 1 with 49 votes when Spanish film magazine *[Nickel Odeon](/w/index.php?title=Nickel_Odeon_(magazine)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Nickel Odeon (magazine) (page does not exist)") [[es](https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nickel_Odeon_(revista) "es:Nickel Odeon (revista)")]* asked 150 Spanish film experts to vote for the best films in 1999.[[7]](#cite_note-7)
* *[Seven Samurai](/wiki/Seven_Samurai "Seven Samurai")* (1954) was voted the greatest foreign-language (non-English) film in [BBC](/wiki/BBC "BBC")'s [2018 poll](/wiki/BBC%27s_100_Greatest_Foreign-Language_Films "BBC's 100 Greatest Foreign-Language Films") of 209 critics in 43 countries.[[8]](#cite_note-bbc-8)
* [*Vertigo*](/wiki/Vertigo_(film) "Vertigo (film)") (1958) was ranked number 1 with 39 votes when German film magazine *[Steadycam](/w/index.php?title=Steadycam_(magazine)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Steadycam (magazine) (page does not exist)") [[de](https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Steadycam_(Filmzeitschrift) "de:Steadycam (Filmzeitschrift)")]* asked 174 critics and filmmakers to vote for their favorite films in 2007.[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10) It was also ranked number 1 with 25 votes when Iranian film magazine *[Film](/wiki/Film_(Iranian_magazine) "Film (Iranian magazine)")* asked 92 Iranian critics to vote for the best films in 2009.[[11]](#cite_note-Film_(Iran)-11) It topped also the *[Télérama](/wiki/T%C3%A9l%C3%A9rama "Télérama")* poll in 2018.[[12]](#cite_note-12)
* *[8½](/wiki/8%C2%BD "8½")* (1963) was voted the best foreign (i.e. non-Swedish) sound film with 21 votes in a 1964 poll of 50 Swedish film professionals organized by Swedish film magazine *[Chaplin](/w/index.php?title=Chaplin_(magazine)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Chaplin (magazine) (page does not exist)") [[sv](https://sv.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chaplin_(filmtidskrift) "sv:Chaplin (filmtidskrift)")]*.[[13]](#cite_note-Chaplin-13) It was also ranked number 1 when the [Museum of Cinematography in Łódź](/w/index.php?title=Museum_of_Cinematography_in_%C5%81%C3%B3d%C5%BA&action=edit&redlink=1 "Museum of Cinematography in Łódź (page does not exist)") [[pl](https://pl.wikipedia.org/wiki/Muzeum_Kinematografii_w_%C5%81odzi "pl:Muzeum Kinematografii w Łodzi")] asked 279 Polish film professionals (filmmakers, critics, and professors) to vote for the best films in 2015.[[14]](#cite_note-Łódź-14)
* *[Gone with the Wind](/wiki/Gone_with_the_Wind_(film) "Gone with the Wind (film)")* (1939) was selected as the greatest film of the past half-century in a 1950 poll conducted by *[Variety](/wiki/Variety_(magazine) "Variety (magazine)")* of more than 200 professionals who worked in the film industry for over 25 years.[[15]](#cite_note-15)
* *[The Godfather](/wiki/The_Godfather "The Godfather")* (1972) was ranked number 1 when Japanese film magazine *[Kinema Junpo](/wiki/Kinema_Junpo "Kinema Junpo")* asked 114 Japanese critics and film professionals to vote for the best foreign (i.e. non-Japanese) films in 2009.[[16]](#cite_note-kinejun1-16) It was also voted the greatest film in a *[Hollywood Reporter](/wiki/The_Hollywood_Reporter "The Hollywood Reporter")* poll of 2120 industry members, including every studio, agency, publicity firm and production house in Hollywood in 2014.[[17]](#cite_note-hollywood-17)
* *[Boyz n the Hood](/wiki/Boyz_n_the_Hood "Boyz n the Hood")* (1991) topped the "Top Black Films of All Time" poll from the November 1998 edition of *[Ebony](/wiki/Ebony_(magazine) "Ebony (magazine)")* magazine.[[18]](#cite_note-18)
* *[The Piano](/wiki/The_Piano "The Piano")* (1993) was voted the best film made by a [female director](/wiki/Female_directors "Female directors") in a 2019 [BBC](/wiki/BBC "BBC") poll of 368 film experts from 84 countries.[[19]](#cite_note-19)

## Audience polls

* *[Gone with the Wind](/wiki/Gone_with_the_Wind_(film) "Gone with the Wind (film)")* (1939) was voted the favorite film of Americans in a poll of 2,279 adults taken by [Harris Interactive](/wiki/Harris_Insights_%26_Analytics "Harris Insights & Analytics") in 2008,[[20]](#cite_note-Harris_2008-20) and again in a follow-up poll of 2,276 adults in 2014.[[21]](#cite_note-Harris_2014-21)
* *[Roman Holiday](/wiki/Roman_Holiday "Roman Holiday")* (1953) was voted the best foreign (i.e. non-Japanese) film of all time in a 1990 poll of about a million people organized by Japanese public broadcaster [NHK](/wiki/NHK "NHK").[[22]](#cite_note-NHK-22)
* *[The Godfather](/wiki/The_Godfather "The Godfather")* (1972) was voted number 1 by *[Entertainment Weekly](/wiki/Entertainment_Weekly "Entertainment Weekly")*'s readers in 1999[[23]](#cite_note-23) and voted as number 1 in a *[Time Out](/wiki/Time_Out_(magazine) "Time Out (magazine)")* readers' poll in 1998.[[24]](#cite_note-24) The film was voted the "Greatest Movie of All Time" in September 2008 by 10,000 readers of *[Empire](/wiki/Empire_(film_magazine) "Empire (film magazine)")* magazine, 150 people from the movie business, and 50 film critics.[[25]](#cite_note-25) It also topped *Empire*'s June 2017 poll of 20,000 readers.[[26]](#cite_note-26)[[27]](#cite_note-27)
* *[The Empire Strikes Back](/wiki/The_Empire_Strikes_Back "The Empire Strikes Back")* (1980) was voted the best film of all time by over 250,000 readers of the *Empire* film magazine in 2015.[[28]](#cite_note-28)
* *[Himala](/wiki/Himala "Himala")* (*Miracle*, 1982) won the 2008 CNN Asia Pacific Screen Awards Viewers Choice as "Best Asia-Pacific Film of All Time" (voted for by thousands of film fans around the world).[[29]](#cite_note-29)
* *[The Shawshank Redemption](/wiki/The_Shawshank_Redemption "The Shawshank Redemption")* (1994) was voted the greatest film of all time by *Empire* readers in "The 201 Greatest Movies of All Time" poll taken in March 2006.[[30]](#cite_note-30)
* [*Titanic*](/wiki/Titanic_(1997_film) "Titanic (1997 film)") (1997) was voted the greatest hit of all time in a poll of 6,000 movie fans conducted by English-language newspaper *[China Daily](/wiki/China_Daily "China Daily")* in March 2008.[[31]](#cite_note-31)
* [*Shiri*](/wiki/Shiri_(film) "Shiri (film)") (1999) was voted the favorite film of South Koreans with 11,918 votes in a 2002 online poll of 54,013 people conducted by South Korean movie channel [Orion Cinema Network](/wiki/Orion_Cinema_Network "Orion Cinema Network").[[32]](#cite_note-OCN-32)
* [The *Lord of the Rings* trilogy](/wiki/The_Lord_of_the_Rings_(film_series) "The Lord of the Rings (film series)") (2001–2003)—despite being a series of three films—was voted the favorite "film" of Australians in an audience poll for the Australian television special *[My Favourite Film](/wiki/My_Favourite_Film "My Favourite Film")* in 2005.[[33]](#cite_note-33) It was also voted the best film in a poll of 120,000 German voters for the TV special *Die besten Filme aller Zeiten* ("The best films of all time") in 2004.[[34]](#cite_note-34)

### Action

* *[Mad Max 2](/wiki/Mad_Max_2 "Mad Max 2")* (1981) was voted the greatest [action film](/wiki/Action_film "Action film") of all time in a readers' poll by American magazine *[Rolling Stone](/wiki/Rolling_Stone "Rolling Stone")* in 2015.[[35]](#cite_note-35)

### Animation (shorts and features)

* *[Hedgehog in the Fog](/wiki/Hedgehog_in_the_Fog "Hedgehog in the Fog")* (1975) was ranked number 1 in a poll at the 2003 Laputa Animation Festival where 140 animators from around the world voted for the best animated films of all time.[[40]](#cite_note-40)[[41]](#cite_note-41)
* *[Tale of Tales](/wiki/Tale_of_Tales_(1979_film) "Tale of Tales (1979 film)")* (1979) was ranked number 1 with 17 votes in a poll at the Olympiad of Animation in 1984 where an international panel of 35 journalists, scholars, festival directors, and animation programmers voted for the best animated films.[[42]](#cite_note-42)[[43]](#cite_note-Tale_of_Tales-43) It was also ranked number 1 in a poll organized by the [Channel 4](/wiki/Channel_4 "Channel 4") animation magazine *Dope Sheet* in 1997, as well as a poll organized by the [Zagreb International Animation Festival](/wiki/Animafest_Zagreb "Animafest Zagreb"), which announced the results in 2002.[[43]](#cite_note-Tale_of_Tales-43)
* *[Nausicaä of the Valley of the Wind](/wiki/Nausica%C3%A4_of_the_Valley_of_the_Wind_(film) "Nausicaä of the Valley of the Wind (film)")* (1984) was the highest-ranking film in a 2006 poll of the greatest animations conducted at the [Japan Media Arts Festival](/wiki/Japan_Media_Arts_Festival "Japan Media Arts Festival"), voted by 80,000 attendees.[[44]](#cite_note-JMA-44)

Voters chose from a list of 10 English-language films selected by film industry experts.[[211]](#cite_note-211)
* *[Citizen Kane](/wiki/Citizen_Kane "Citizen Kane")* (1941) was selected as the greatest American film in 2015 by sixty-two international film critics surveyed by the BBC.[[212]](#cite_note-212) It was also ranked top in every *Sight & Sound* critics' poll between 1962 and 2002, and the directors' poll in 1992 and 2002.[[1]](#cite_note-S&S-1) The [American Film Institute](/wiki/American_Film_Institute "American Film Institute") polled 1,500 film community leaders for the lists *[100 Years...100 Movies](/wiki/AFI%27s_100_Years...100_Movies "AFI's 100 Years...100 Movies")* and *[the 10th Anniversary Edition](/wiki/AFI%27s_100_Years...100_Movies_(10th_Anniversary_Edition) "AFI's 100 Years...100 Movies (10th Anniversary Edition)")* in 1998 and 2007 respectively, asking voters to choose from a list of 400 nominations. Both polls identified *Citizen Kane* as the best [American film](/wiki/Category:American_films "Category:American films") ever.[[213]](#cite_note-213)[[214]](#cite_note-214) It was voted the best American film of all time with 156 votes in a 1977 poll of 203 experts from 22 countries (116 Americans and 87 non-Americans). The poll was organized by the [Royal Belgian Film Archive](/wiki/Cinematek "Cinematek") and titled "The most important and misappreciated American films", and they were looking for subjective choices.[[215]](#cite_note-215)
* *[Casablanca](/wiki/Casablanca_(film) "Casablanca (film)")* (1942) was voted the greatest American film by readers of the *[Los Angeles Daily News](/wiki/Los_Angeles_Daily_News "Los Angeles Daily News")* in 1997.[[216]](#cite_note-216)
* *[Vertigo](/wiki/Vertigo_(film) "Vertigo (film)")* (1958) topped the *Sight & Sound* critics' poll in 2012 with 191 votes.[[1]](#cite_note-S&S-1)
* *[The Godfather](/wiki/The_Godfather "The Godfather")* (1972) was selected as the greatest film by 2,120 industry professionals in a [Hollywood](/wiki/Cinema_of_the_United_States "Cinema of the United States") survey undertaken by *[The Hollywood Reporter](/wiki/The_Hollywood_Reporter "The Hollywood Reporter")* in 2014.[[17]](#cite_note-hollywood-17)

### Uruguay

See also: [Cinema of Uruguay](/wiki/Cinema_of_Uruguay "Cinema of Uruguay")

* [*Whisky*](/wiki/Whisky_(film) "Whisky (film)") (2004) was voted the best Uruguayan film of all time by 22 members of the [Uruguayan Film Critics Association](/wiki/Category:Uruguayan_Film_Critics_Association_Awards "Category:Uruguayan Film Critics Association Awards") in 2015.[[217]](#cite_note-217)

### Venezuela

See also: [Cinema of Venezuela](/wiki/Cinema_of_Venezuela "Cinema of Venezuela")

* *[El Pez que Fuma](/wiki/El_Pez_que_Fuma "El Pez que Fuma")* (1977) was voted the best Venezuelan film of all time with 22 votes in a 1987 poll of 29 experts organized by *Imagen* magazine.[[218]](#cite_note-Fundación_Cinemateca_Nacional-218) It was also voted the best Venezuelan film of all time with 33 votes in a 2016 poll of 41 experts organized by the [Fundación Cinemateca Nacional](/wiki/Cinemateca_Nacional_de_Venezuela "Cinemateca Nacional de Venezuela").[[218]](#cite_note-Fundación_Cinemateca_Nacional-218)
